run_summary: Screen 1 evaluates two new OVERDONE flags (EVH, DUOL) against four open positions all trading at 4–6 days held with positive thesis momentum. EVH clears the buy bar as exploratory; DUOL sits at the confidence threshold with 'indirect' threat and is sized exploratory if admitted. All open positions are held—sympathy fade intact, no fresh negatives, days_held well within window.

entries.name: Evolent Health, Inc
entries.catalyst: Anthropic launches Claude Sonnet 5 as a cheaper way to run agents
entries.thesis: Evolent's product is regulated payer contracts plus specialty clinical networks plus risk-sharing capital — a cheaper Anthropic agent model does not displace a Medicaid managed-care contract or an oncology risk corridor. The down move on an Anthropic agentic-model day is thematic SaaS-sympathy selling against a name whose real risks (Medicaid redeterminations, oncology cost trend, OBBBA policy changes) have nothing to do with LLM pricing.
entries.what_kills: A subsequent customer-loss 8-K or downward guide tied to a specific payer renegotiation — those would reveal the move was idiosyncratic, not sympathy.
entries.reasoning: Classification OVERDONE, confidence 3 (meets min_buy_confidence). Threat_assessment 'minimal' (AI/ML flagged as general tech risk, not substitution threat to payer contracts and specialty networks). Panic_calibration 'unjustified'—filings evidence shows EVH's revenue model (contract-driven Medicaid managed care, clinical risk-sharing, CMS-regulated value-based care) is mechanically untouched by cheaper Claude agentic agents. The down move is thematic SaaS sympathy, not idiosyncratic impairment. 5-day move +12.92% shows partial recovery already underway, supporting sympathy-fade thesis. Confidence 3 (threshold) + 'minimal' threat (not 'none') + emerging recovery = exploratory tier. Positioned as 6% of equity.

red_team.weakest_link: The bull case depends on EVH's core business being mechanically insulated from AI displacement, but provides no evidence that the market recognizes this distinction or that the sympathy fade will reverse.
red_team.critique: The bull thesis rests on a correct observation—that payer contracts and clinical networks are not threatened by cheaper agentic models—but conflates market logic with fundamental resilience. The 5-day recovery of +12.92% is cited as evidence of 'sympathy-fade thesis,' yet that same recovery is equally consistent with a temporary oversold bounce in a name whose risk profile has genuinely shifted. The thesis names no mechanism by which the market will re-rate EVH's insulation from AI displacement; it assumes the market will simply 'see through' the SaaS sympathy without evidence. The real kill condition ('customer-loss 8-K') is set so high that ordinary competitive pressures or margin pressure from payer pushback could easily persist without triggering it, leaving the position vulnerable to a slower grind lower.

entries.name: Duolingo, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Gemini's personalized AI image generation now free for US users
entries.thesis: Today's image-gen announcement is functionally irrelevant to Duolingo's product (text/audio-driven habit loop with gamification). Retail reflexively sells edtech on any frontier-AI release, but Gemini personalized image generation is not the LLM-tutor threat that would actually erode DUOL's TAM. The moat is the dopamine/streak loop, not content uniqueness, and DUOL is also already extended down on the week — sympathy compounding rather than fundamentals.
entries.what_kills: Google announces a Gemini-powered language tutor directly competing with DUOL; DAU growth visibly decelerates in the next quarterly print; Duolingo Max subscriber metrics disappoint.
entries.reasoning: Classification OVERDONE, confidence 3 (meets min_buy_confidence). However, threat_assessment is 'indirect'—filings evidence distinguishes between image-gen (today's Gemini announcement) and LLM-tutor threat (real competitive risk to DUOL). The panic is arguably unjustified (image-gen is not DUOL's actual threat vector), but 'indirect' threat + already-extended 5-day move (-8.82%) + ambiguous filings signal (DUOL has integrated GenAI itself via Max, but also acknowledges tutoring-model risk) make this borderline. The thesis read is sound (image-gen irrelevant), but the leverage to conviction is thin. WATCH to track whether DUOL Max metrics stabilize in coming days; if filings language clarifies the tutoring-model exposure further, revisit as exploratory BUY.
